refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions to establish traceability.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency and control responsiveness sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator control with latency, video fidelity, and feedback quality sufficient to achieve productivity_target when operating in tele-operated mode.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and operational policies without reducing productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time telemetry and task status to the Site Manager and Tele-operator to enable informed operational decisions and task scheduling.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-performance hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with direct operator controls and remote teleoperation capability; implement real-time telemetry streaming and safety constraint enforcement via centralized control logic.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with pluggable operator interface (on-site joystick or remote console); implement productivity monitoring via task-completion counters and time-tracking; enforce safety via independent safety controller.

DP-C: Architect a distributed system where vehicle actuation, teleoperation, safety enforcement, and telemetry are independently scalable microservices; use event-driven messaging for inter-service communication and safety state synchronization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate a high-performance hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with direct operator controls and remote teleoperation capability; implement real-time telemetry streaming and safety constraint enforcement via centralized control logic.

potential_sources: Vehicle mechanical performance (bucket capacity, cycle time, fuel efficiency) is coupled to operator control responsiveness; both must be optimized together to achieve productivity_target.
Teleoperation latency and video quality are coupled to network infrastructure and vehicle sensor suite; poor network or inadequate sensors will degrade remote operator effectiveness.
Safety constraint enforcement (e.g., geofencing, load limits) is coupled to vehicle control logic; overly restrictive safety rules may reduce productivity below target.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A acknowledges these couplings explicitly and proposes centralized control logic to manage them. This is appropriate for a tightly integrated system where productivity and safety must be co-optimized. DP-B and DP-C introduce additional decoupling (independent safety controller, microservices) which may reduce coupling but increase complexity and latency—less suitable for high-productivity earthmoving where responsiveness is critical.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator skill and training are adequate; productivity_target is achievable with competent operators under nominal conditions.
Assumption: Network infrastructure (bandwidth, latency, reliability) is available for teleoperation; if not, on-site operation mode must be primary.
Risk: Centralized control logic becomes a single point of failure; if control system fails, both on-site and remote operation are lost.
Risk: Safety constraint enforcement via centralized logic may introduce latency that degrades operator responsiveness and productivity.
Risk: Teleoperation latency (network + processing) may exceed operator tolerance, making remote operation impractical for fine-grained tasks.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the acceptable latency budget for teleoperation (e.g., <500 ms round-trip) and the fallback mode if network is unavailable (e.g., on-site operation only). Define the safety constraint enforcement strategy (e.g., hard limits vs. warnings) and its impact on productivity. Specify the vehicle's mechanical performance targets (bucket capacity, cycle time, fuel efficiency) that are necessary to achieve productivity_target.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system integration boundary that encompasses all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their coordination. Responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery, telemetry aggregation, and safety policy enforcement across all operational modes.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time stream of vehicle state (position, orientation, bucket load, fuel level, engine RPM, hydraulic pressure) and task progress (m³ moved, ton loaded, cycle count) sent to Site Manager and Tele-operator dashboards.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Task commands from Site Manager or Tele-operator (e.g., 'move to location X', 'dig at location Y', 'load material type Z') that specify the earthmoving operation to be executed.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical alerts (e.g., geofence violation, load limit exceeded, operator override, emergency stop) sent to Safety Manager and on-site personnel.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional network connection for teleoperation commands (operator input), telemetry streaming, and safety alert transmission. Supports both on-site (local network) and remote (WAN) operation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary and is the natural home for the refined FR. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within it. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle module contains mechanical actuation and on-site operator controls, but does NOT contain teleoperation logic, safety enforcement, or telemetry aggregation. Assigning the full FR to Vehicle would require Vehicle to own interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) that logically belong to the system integration layer. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation module handles remote operator control and video/feedback, but does NOT own vehicle actuation, on-site operation, or safety enforcement. Assigning the full FR to Teleoperation would exclude on-site operation mode and safety constraints. Significant diffusion and incomplete coverage.

Safety Services module enforces safety constraints, but does NOT own vehicle actuation, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning the full FR to Safety Services would conflate safety enforcement with productivity delivery. High diffusion and misalignment with FR intent.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The refined FR (productivity delivery across on-site and remote operation modes, with safety compliance) is best contained within the NGHE Solution boundary because: (1) it requires coordination of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (2) it spans multiple operational modes and interfaces; (3) it is a system-level acceptance criterion, not a component-level requirement. Assigning it to any sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) would fragment ownership and create diffusion across multiple modules.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary; assigning the FR here may defer detailed design decisions to lower levels, potentially creating ambiguity about which sub-module is responsible for specific aspects of productivity (e.g., vehicle efficiency vs. operator responsiveness).

Risk: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services are developed by different teams or vendors, coordination of productivity targets across modules may be difficult. Clear interface contracts and performance budgets are essential.

Risk: Productivity measurement and verification require instrumentation (telemetry) and data collection across multiple modules; if telemetry is incomplete or unreliable, productivity claims cannot be validated.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear performance budgets for each sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) that collectively enable the NGHE Solution to achieve productivity_target. For example: Vehicle must achieve cycle time ≤ X seconds; Teleoperation must achieve latency ≤ Y ms; Safety Services must enforce constraints with <Z% false positives. Define telemetry requirements (what data must be collected, at what frequency, with what accuracy) to enable productivity measurement and verification. Establish ownership and interface contracts between NGHE Solution and each sub-module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (integrated vehicle with teleoperation and centralized control logic) directly addresses the refined FR (productivity delivery across on-site and remote modes with safety compliance). The DP specifies a coherent solution approach: high-performance vehicle + remote capability + real-time telemetry + safety enforcement. No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Centralized control logic (specified in DP) must be implemented across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. If control logic is duplicated or inconsistently implemented across modules, safety and productivity may be compromised.

Diffusion Risk 2: Real-time telemetry streaming (specified in DP) requires data collection from Vehicle, processing in Safety Services, and transmission via Network Link. If telemetry is fragmented across modules, end-to-end latency may exceed acceptable limits.

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety constraint enforcement (specified in DP) is centralized, but Vehicle and Teleoperation modules must respect these constraints. If modules bypass or override constraints, safety may be compromised.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it owns the system-level integration logic (control coordination, telemetry aggregation, safety enforcement) that the DP specifies. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and must implement their portions of the DP. Clear interface contracts and performance budgets are needed to manage diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR (productivity ≥ productivity_target) is feasible via representative operations trials. Telemetry from NGHE Solution provides the data needed to measure productivity (m³/h or ton/h). Verification requires: (1) instrumentation to collect telemetry; (2) test plan defining representative operations; (3) acceptance criteria (productivity_target, evaluation period, environmental conditions); (4) traceability from test results to FR satisfaction.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The refined FR is allocated to NGHE Solution, which is responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery. Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (mechanical efficiency), Teleoperation (remote control quality), Safety Services (constraint enforcement), and NGHE Solution (telemetry and coordination). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Define productivity_target as a specific numerical value (e.g., 150 m³/h for excavation, 200 ton/h for loading) and specify the evaluation period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 40-hour week) and environmental conditions (soil type, weather, operator experience).

Revision 2: Establish performance budgets for Vehicle (cycle time, fuel efficiency), Teleoperation (latency, video quality), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement overhead) that collectively enable NGHE Solution to achieve productivity_target.

Revision 3: Define telemetry requirements (data types, collection frequency, accuracy, latency) needed to measure and verify productivity. Specify data ownership and access rights (Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Manager, Maintenance).
Revision 4: Clarify the fallback mode if teleoperation is unavailable (e.g., on-site operation only) and the impact on productivity_target.
Revision 5: Define the safety constraint enforcement strategy (hard limits vs. warnings) and its impact on productivity. Specify acceptable latency for safety constraint enforcement.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-formed, the selected DP is coherent and addresses the FR, and the assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate for system-level integration. However, the FR is not atomic; it encompasses multiple operational modes (on-site and remote), multiple stakeholders (Operator, Tele-operator, Site Manager, Safety Manager), and multiple concerns (productivity, safety, telemetry). Decomposition to the next level is necessary to allocate responsibilities to sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and to define detailed design parameters for each sub-module. The sub-FRs identified in the refined_fr section provide a clear roadmap for the next decomposition level.
decision.justification_detail: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that serves as the top-level system requirement. The refined FR clarifies the intent and scope, but is still too broad for detailed design. The sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) are more specific and can be assigned to individual modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) for further decomposition. Proceeding to the next level will enable detailed design of each sub-module and clear definition of interfaces and performance budgets.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency and control responsiveness sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator control with latency, video fidelity, and feedback quality sufficient to achieve productivity_target when operating in tele-operated mode.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and operational policies without reducing productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time telemetry and task status to the Site Manager and Tele-operator to enable informed operational decisions and task scheduling.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Operator skill and training are adequate; productivity_target is achievable with competent operators under nominal conditions.
Assumption 2: Network infrastructure (bandwidth, latency, reliability) is available for teleoperation; if not, on-site operation mode is primary.
Assumption 3: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and can be encoded as constraints in the Safety Services module.
Assumption 4: Productivity is measured as m³/h or ton/h based on telemetry data collected from the Vehicle and aggregated by NGHE Solution.
Assumption 5: Representative operations trials are feasible and representative of actual customer use cases.
open_risks: Risk 1: Centralized control logic becomes a single point of failure; if control system fails, both on-site and remote operation are lost. Mitigation: Implement redundancy and failover mechanisms.

Risk 2: Teleoperation latency (network + processing) may exceed operator tolerance, making remote operation impractical for fine-grained tasks. Mitigation: Define acceptable latency budget and validate via user trials.

Risk 3: Safety constraint enforcement via centralized logic may introduce latency that degrades operator responsiveness and productivity. Mitigation: Optimize constraint enforcement logic and validate via performance testing.

Risk 4: Productivity measurement and verification require reliable telemetry; if telemetry is incomplete or unreliable, productivity claims cannot be validated. Mitigation: Implement redundant telemetry channels and data validation.

Risk 5: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are developed by different teams or vendors, coordination of productivity targets across modules may be difficult. Mitigation: Establish clear interface contracts and performance budgets.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Specification of productivity_target (numerical value, evaluation period, environmental conditions).
Evidence 2: Performance budgets for Vehicle (cycle time, fuel efficiency), Teleoperation (latency, video quality), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement overhead).
Evidence 3: Telemetry requirements (data types, collection frequency, accuracy, latency).
Evidence 4: Test plan for representative operations trials, including test scenarios, acceptance criteria, and traceability to FR.
Evidence 5: Interface specifications for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link, including data formats, protocols, and performance requirements.
Evidence 6: Safety constraint definitions and enforcement strategy (hard limits vs. warnings).
Evidence 7: Fallback mode specification (e.g., on-site operation only if teleoperation is unavailable).
